---
title: "SEEC Publications"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(DT)

# Option A: If you already have a dataframe named `pubs` in the environment,
# comment out the CSV section below.

# Option B: Read from a CSV (recommended)
source("R/data.R")
d <- seec_read_data()

# Basic cleaning / type safety
pubs <- d$pubs %>%
  mutate(
    year  = suppressWarnings(as.integer(year)),
    cites = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(cites))
  )
```

## Yearly summary table 

```{r}
pubs_yearly <- pubs %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    publications   = n(),
    total_citations = sum(cites, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_citations  = round(mean(cites, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    journals        = n_distinct(journal),
    highly_cited_100 = sum(cites >= 100, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(year))

kable(
  pubs_yearly,
  col.names = c(
    "Year",
    "Publications",
    "Total citations",
    "Mean citations",
    "Distinct journals",
    "Highly cited (≥100)"
  ),
  caption = "Summary of SEEC-affiliated publications by year"
)
```

## Publications per year

```{r}
ggplot(pubs_yearly, aes(x = year, y = publications)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of publications",
    title = "SEEC-affiliated publications per year"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Yearly highlights (top 3 most cited per year)

```{r}
top_by_year <- pubs %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%
  arrange(year, desc(cites)) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  slice_head(n = 3) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(year, title, journal, cites) %>%
  arrange(desc(year), desc(cites))

kable(
  top_by_year,
  col.names = c("Year", "Title", "Journal", "Citations"),
  caption = "Top 3 most cited publications per year"
)
```

---

## Full publications table 

```{r}
DT::datatable(
  pubs %>% arrange(desc(year), desc(cites)),
  options = list(pageLength = 25, scrollX = TRUE),
  rownames = FALSE
)
```
